chunks.text: Course: CS 160 | EECS at UC Berkeley CS 160. User Interface Design and Development Catalog Description: The design, implementation, and evaluation of user interfaces. User-centered design and task analysis. Conceptual models and interface metaphors. Usability inspection and evaluation methods. Analysis of user study data. Input methods (keyboard, pointing, touch, tangible) and input models. Visual design principles. Interface prototyping and implementation methodologies and tools. Students will develop a user interface for a specific task and target user group in teams. Units: 4 Also Offered As: COMPSCI 160 Related Areas: Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) Prerequisites: COMPSCI 61B or COMPSCI 61BL. Formats: Summer: 6.0 hours of lecture and 2.0 hours of discussion per week Spring: 3.0 hours of lecture and 1.0 hours of discussion per week Fall: 3.0 hours of lecture and 1.0 hours of discussion per week Grading Basis: Student Option Final Exam Status: Yes Links: EECS Course Archives (CalNet authentication required) CS enrollment policies EE enrollment policies HKN course maps
